title: odontites viscosus subsp. australis
intro: Odontites viscosus subsp. australis, also known as sticky toothwort or Australian odontites, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family, known for its sticky glandular hairs and fragrant, purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Odontites viscosus subsp. australis include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
